assembler.case_sensitivity: All identifiers, mnemonics, directives, and registers are case sensitive.

cli_usage.exit_behavior: On error: non-zero exit; prints file:line, the failing line, and an include stack.

comments.rule: Everything after ';' on a line is a comment.

.byte.allowed_values: Numeric literals only (decimal/hex/binary). Labels are not allowed.
.byte.behavior: Emits each value as one byte.

behavior: Location counter (LC) starts at 0x0003 (0x0000..0x0002 reserved for implicit entry JMP).
.org with no operand marks an entry point at current LC and does not change LC (allowed at most once).
.org <addr> sets LC to <addr> (numeric literal only; labels are not allowed).
constraints: At least one .org (either form) must appear in the program.
.org <addr> must be a numeric literal in [0x0003..0xFFFF].
Overlapping writes across segments are forbidden.
Only one entry marker (.org with no operand) may exist.
entry_selection: If an entry marker exists, entry = its LC.
Otherwise, entry = address from the first .org <addr> encountered.

behavior: Decodes a C-like string literal into bytes and emits them.
.string.charset: After escape decoding, all bytes must be <= 0x7F (7-bit ASCII).

.word.behavior: Emits each value as two bytes: high then low.

behavior: Textual include (the line is replaced by the included file contents).
Paths are resolved relative to the including file.
Include-once is enforced: including the exact same file more than once is an error.

labels.placement: Can be on its own line or before an instruction/directive on the same line.
labels.scope: Single global namespace across all files after preprocessing.

lessons_learned.example_good:
LDI A, #0x2A   ; immediate
JMP loop        ; address via label
lessons_learned.lesson: Use '#' only for immediates. Bare numbers/labels are addresses. Mixing them is a common source of wrong encoding.
.org with an operand moves the location counter. '.org' with no operand is treated as an entry-point marker (and may appear only once). The operand must be a numeric literal (labels not allowed).
lessons_learned.pitfall: Trying to write '.org start' or '.org #0x200' will fail by design.
lessons_learned.reason: Keeps entry-point explicit and avoids forward-reference ambiguity for the image layout.

lessons_learned.lesson: .byte accepts numeric literals only (no labels, no '#'). .word accepts numeric literals or labels (no '#'). Keep data widths explicit to avoid accidental truncation.
lessons_learned.pitfall: Using labels in .byte or using immediates in .word/.byte.

lessons_learned.lesson: Operands are split by commas without quote awareness. Keep string directives as a single quoted argument and avoid commas inside quoted strings unless the directive explicitly supports it.
lessons_learned.prevention: If you need commas in emitted bytes, use multiple .byte items rather than a single .string with commas.

lessons_learned.lesson: Labels are global and duplicates are a hard error. This prevents silent symbol capture across includes.
lessons_learned.prevention: Use a consistent prefixing convention per module (e.g., 'mem_', 'fmt_', 'cli_').

lessons_learned.lesson: .include requires quotes and is resolved relative to the including file (with canonical/absolute handling). Keep includes acyclic; include stacks are reported on errors.
lessons_learned.pitfall: Forgetting quotes or relying on working-directory relative paths.

lessons_learned.lesson: Any emitted byte overlap is a strict error. This catches accidental .org jumps into already-emitted regions early.
lessons_learned.prevention: Treat .org as a segment boundary and keep a simple memory map comment at the top of your entry file.

lessons_learned.lesson: Pass 1 builds layout and label addresses; pass 2 emits. Forward references are fine for instructions and .word, but not allowed where the assembler requires literal-only operands (e.g., .org operand, .byte).
lessons_learned.prevention: When in doubt, choose .word for label-bearing data and keep control directives literal.

lessons_learned.lesson: Preserve the original line (before comment stripping) for diagnostics, and report file/line plus include stack. Good errors reduce debugging time more than any other feature.
lessons_learned.note: If you extend the assembler, keep new errors consistent with AsmError formatting.

lessons_learned.lesson: CMP/CMPR subtract into the compared register. Never compare a live value in-place if it is still needed afterward; compare on a scratch copy instead.
lessons_learned.reason: This caused real stack/index corruption bugs in BASIC control-flow code.

lessons_learned.lesson: Treat decimal 38400/38401 (0x9600/0x9601) as reserved BASIC scratch/test-safe addresses in the current project layout.
lessons_learned.pitfall: Allowing interpreter code growth into that range means user POKE can overwrite instructions and produce late, hard-to-debug failures.
lessons_learned.prevention: Check the final .deb map after code growth and preserve the gap/relocation strategy unless the tests and memory map are intentionally redesigned.

lessons_learned.lesson: The .pre.s8.asm file explains final include order and effective source, while the .deb file explains exact emitted addresses and source mapping.
lessons_learned.prevention: When behavior changes after a refactor or new feature, inspect both outputs before assuming the parser or VM is wrong.

lessons_learned.topic: BASIC block-loop scans must preserve caller scratch
lessons_learned.lesson: Runtime structure scans for BASIC DO/WHILE/ENDWHILE must preserve loop counters and scan state across helper calls. On Sophia8, helpers freely clobber registers unless you save them explicitly.
lessons_learned.pitfall: Using R5/R6/R7 as scan counters and then calling token/classifier helpers without saving them can make matching skip or jump to the wrong line.

immediates.rule: Immediate operands must start with '#' and must fit in 8 bits (0..255).

debug_map.description: Text debug map: address/len/kind(CODE|DATA)/bytes + file:line: original source line. Used by VM for breakpoints.

preprocessed_source.description: Fully preprocessed source (all .include expanded). Each line is preceded by ';@ file:line' origin marker.

source_format.encoding: UTF-8 source, but .string must decode to 7-bit ASCII bytes (0x00..0x7F).

features: RUN and packed variable-length line-numbered program storage
REM comments and apostrophe (') comment shorthand
PRINT item lists with ';' and ',' separators
INPUT for one or more variables, with optional quoted prompt strings
String concatenation with '+' (PRINT and string assignment)

architecture.top_level_role: Composition/wiring only: core libs, fixed BASIC strings/state, aggregate include, entry point.
architecture.rule: Implement features in focused basic_*.s8.asm modules and wire them through basic_all.s8.asm instead of growing the top-level BASIC file.

binary_image.overlap: Forbidden. Writing two different bytes to the same address is an assembler error.

gotchas: CMP/CMPR are destructive (they subtract into the compared register).
DIV/DIVR overwrite the quotient register before writing remainder; do not use the same register for both outputs.
Include-once is enforced (including the same file twice is an assembler error).
.org operands must be numeric literals (no labels).
MUL/MULR destination register order: encoded as (dst_high, dst_low). If you want low in R6 and high in R7, write: MUL #imm, R7, R6.
Preserve 0x9600/0x9601 as reserved BASIC scratch/test-safe addresses unless the layout is intentionally redesigned.
layout: Keep all user code/data at addresses >= 0x0003.
If you include kernel.s8.asm or other libs, place them explicitly with .org <addr> before including.
Avoid overlap: never emit code/data into the same address twice.
Inspect the generated .deb map after meaningful code growth; address safety is a layout property, not an assumption.
For the current BASIC layout, keep interpreter code away from 0x9600/0x9601 because tests intentionally POKE there.
strict: Never invent new instructions, directives, or registers.
Do not assume macros like .equ exist (they do not).

context_pack_name: Sophia8 Assembler + ISA + Standard Libraries Context Pack

flags.note: There is no exposed flags register in assembly; only the JC/JNC branch on the internal carry flag.

special.access: Only usable as operands of PUSH/POP (s8asm restriction).

stack.byte_order_for_16bit_push: big-endian: high byte at [SP], low byte at [SP+1] after PUSH
stack.width: Byte-addressed; PUSH/POP of IP/SP/BP move 2 bytes; CALL/RET move 2 bytes.

intent: Authoritative, implementation-accurate reference for Sophia8 assembly as accepted by s8asm and executed by the sophia8 VM.
Do not assume anything that is not explicitly described here.

isa.instruction_encoding: First byte is opcode; remaining bytes are operands as described.

instructions.effects: Stops the VM.
No operation (IP += 1).
Rn = imm8
Rn = mem[addr16]
mem[addr16] = Rn
Rdst = mem[(Rhi<<8)|Rlo]
mem[(Rhi<<8)|Rlo] = Rsrc
Rn++ (8-bit wrap). Carry set to 1 if value wrapped from 0xFF to 0x00.
Rn-- (8-bit wrap). Carry set to 1 if value wrapped from 0x00 to 0xFF.
Rn = Rn + imm8 (8-bit wrap). Carry=1 on overflow.
Rdst = Rdst + Rsrc (8-bit wrap). Carry=1 on overflow.
Rn = Rn - imm8 (8-bit wrap). Carry=1 on borrow (Rn < imm8).
Rdst = Rdst - Rsrc (8-bit wrap). Carry=1 on borrow (Rdst < Rsrc).

effects: Let X = original value of Rq.
Let D = value of Rsrc, X = original value of Rq.
Rq = X / D; Rr = X % D.
Carry is set to the last bit shifted out (per VM implementation; imm8 must be >=1 for meaningful carry).
Carry is set to the last bit shifted out (per VM implementation; imm8 must be >=1 for meaningful carry).

instructions.effects: IP = addr16
If Rn == 0 then IP = addr16 else IP += 4
If Rn != 0 then IP = addr16 else IP += 4
If carry==1 then IP = addr16 else IP += 3
If carry==0 then IP = addr16 else IP += 3

effects: If operand is R0..R7: pushes 1 byte.
If operand is IP/SP/BP: pushes 2 bytes (big-endian).
If operand is R0..R7: pops 1 byte.
If operand is IP/SP/BP: pops 2 bytes (big-endian).

instructions.effects: Pushes 16-bit return address (IP+3), then IP=addr16
Pops 16-bit return address into IP

basic_strfn.s8.asm.purpose: BASIC string functions: LEN, LEFT$, RIGHT$, MID$, ASC, CHR$, INSTR, VAL; plus parsing helpers.
notes: Placed at 0x7000 to avoid overlapping the main BASIC code region.
INSTR(hay$, needle$) returns 1-based position; returns 0 if not found; returns 1 if needle is empty.
VAL(s$) parses the signed decimal prefix of the string (leading spaces allowed); returns 0 if no digits.
Do not treat 0x7000 placement as permanently safe without checking the final .deb/.pre.s8.asm outputs after code growth.

basic_data_cmd.s8.asm.purpose: BASIC command handlers for DATA / READ / RESTORE relocated out of the hot main statement region.
notes: Placed high in memory to reduce pressure around the main BASIC interpreter code.
This relocation helps keep 0x9600/0x9601 free for BASIC POKE scratch/tests.

map.meaning: bit0=1 if a byte is available
map.name: KBD_STATUS

map.meaning: reads and consumes a byte, or returns 0x00 if none
map.name: KBD_DATA

map.meaning: write a byte to console output
map.name: TTY_DATA

basic_helpers.s8.asm.purpose: BASIC-specific helpers factored out of sophia_basic_v1.s8.asm (depends on BASIC globals)

routines.behavior: Reads next space-delimited token from BASIC CURPTR into TOKENBUF (0x6880), max 23 chars + NUL.
routines.name: GETTOKEN

routines.behavior: Parses signed decimal integer from BASIC CURPTR into R6:R7 and advances CURPTR.
routines.name: PARSE_INT16

routines.behavior: BASIC wrapper around RNG_NEXT16 using RNG_SEED_PTR.
routines.name: RNG_NEXT

routines.behavior: Reads a line from console with echo and backspace editing. NUL-terminates.
routines.name: READLINE_ECHO

routines.behavior: Copies a space/tab-delimited token into dst (NUL-terminated).
routines.name: READTOKEN

routines.behavior: Parses an unsigned decimal integer 0..255 (checked). Skips leading spaces/tabs.
routines.name: PARSE_U8_DEC

routines.behavior: Write decimal string to buffer, NUL-terminated.
routines.name: U16_TO_DEC_BUF

ctype.s8.asm.purpose: ASCII character classification and case conversion

routines.behavior: True for ASCII letters or digits.
routines.name: ISALNUM

routines.behavior: Prints R0 as two uppercase hex digits.
routines.name: PUTHEX8

routines.behavior: Prints R1:R2 as four uppercase hex digits.
routines.name: PUTHEX16
routines.notes: Preserves original low byte in R7 because PUTHEX8 clobbers R2; PUTC clobbers R3.

routines.behavior: Prints R0 as unsigned decimal without leading zeros.
routines.name: PUTDEC8

routines.behavior: Waits for TTY ready then writes R0 to TTY_DATA (0xFF03).
routines.name: PUTC

routines.behavior: Blocks until KBD_STATUS indicates available, then reads KBD_DATA into R0.
routines.name: GETC

routines.behavior: Prints NUL-terminated string at [R1:R2] using LOADR and PUTC, increments pointer.
routines.name: PUTS

routines.behavior: Reads a line with echo and backspace editing. Always NUL-terminates.
routines.name: READLINE_ECHO

routines.behavior: Fills length bytes at dst with the byte value.
routines.name: MEMSET

routines.behavior: Copies length bytes from src to dst, forward direction.
routines.name: MEMCPY

routines.behavior: Copies length bytes from src to dst, safe for overlapping ranges (chooses forward/backward copy).
routines.name: MEMMOVE

returns.R0: 0 if equal, 0xFF if a<b, 0x01 if a>b

routines.behavior: Finds the first occurrence of a byte in a bounded range.
routines.name: MEMCHR

parse.s8.asm.purpose: Tokenization and checked u8 decimal parsing

routines.behavior: Copies a space/tab-delimited token into dst (NUL-terminated).
routines.name: READTOKEN

routines.behavior: Parses an unsigned decimal integer 0..255 (checked). Skips leading spaces/tabs.
routines.name: PARSE_U8_DEC

rng.s8.asm.purpose: Simple 16-bit RNG helper (extracted from Sophia BASIC)

routines.behavior: Updates 16-bit seed in memory: seed = seed*109 + 89 (mod 65536). Returns (seed & 0x7FFF) in R6:R7. Requires caller to provide ADD16 and MUL16U labels.
routines.name: RNG_NEXT16

routines.behavior: Counts bytes until NUL. Advances R1:R2 while scanning.
routines.name: STRLEN

routines.behavior: Compares strings for equality. Advances both pointers.
routines.name: STREQ

routines.behavior: Copies NUL-terminated string from src to dst (including terminator).
routines.name: STRCPY

routines.behavior: Copies up to R5 bytes; always NUL-terminates if R5>0.
routines.name: STRNCPY

routines.behavior: Lexicographically compares two NUL-terminated strings. Advances pointers.
routines.name: STRCMP

returns.R0: 0 if equal, 0xFF if s1<s2, 0x01 if s1>s2

routines.behavior: Finds first occurrence of a character in a string.
routines.name: STRCHR

text.s8.asm.purpose: Legacy/BASIC-oriented text helpers (prefer ctype.s8.asm/parse.s8.asm/conv.s8.asm for new code)

routines.behavior: Parses an unsigned decimal number; stops on first non-digit; no overflow checking.
routines.name: PARSE_UINT8

routines.behavior: Uppercases an ASCII NUL-terminated string in place.
routines.name: TOUPPER_Z

breakpoint_behavior: On breakpoint hit, VM prints BREAK <file>:<line> (0xADDR).
Then prints registers (R0..R7, IP, SP, BP, C).

debug_snapshot.format: Binary snapshot: magic 'S8DI', version 0x01, registers, IP/SP/BP, carry, then full 0x10000-byte memory image.

cell_format: byte[8]: color (hi nibble FG 0..15, lo nibble BG 0..15)
memory_layout.cell_order: row-major: cell_index = cy*40+cx; cell_addr = base + cell_index*9

graphics.refresh: best-effort ~60 Hz while running; SDL window stays open after stop until Esc; optional PPM snapshot via --gfx-out

renderer.current_backend: SDL fullscreen window from mapped memory
renderer.window_backend_note: Optional PPM snapshot via --gfx-out; future windowed mode can scale to fit while preserving 320x200 aspect ratio (letterbox/pillarbox).

usage.meaning: Loads and runs a raw memory image.
Loads .deb debug map, loads referenced .bin, runs.
Runs and stops when IP reaches address mapped from source location (file:line). Writes debug.img snapshot and prints registers.
Resumes execution from a saved debug snapshot (written on breakpoint).
Resumes from snapshot and can still use .deb mapping for a new breakpoint.
Runs using the .deb debug map and appends a per-instruction trace to verbose.log (flush after each instruction).
Loads a raw .bin image, uses <program.deb> only for source mapping, and writes verbose.log.
Resumes from debug.img, uses <program.deb> for source mapping, and writes verbose.log.
Loads and runs a raw memory image with graphics rendering enabled (reads VRAM at 0x8000).
Runs with graphics enabled and optionally writes the final frame to the specified PPM output file.

per_instruction_fields: source location resolved from .deb (file:line) for the IP when available
memory writes performed by the instruction (addr, old byte, new byte) when any
register dump after execution: R0..R7, IP, SP, BP, C

basic_reserved_scratch.purpose: Project-reserved BASIC scratch/test-safe area commonly used by POKE/PEEK integration tests.
basic_reserved_scratch.status: Convention in the current project layout; not VM-enforced MMIO.
basic_reserved_scratch.lesson: A regression occurred when interpreter code grew into 0x9600/0x9601 and user POKE overwrote instructions. Keep this range free unless the memory layout and tests are redesigned together.
current_mitigation: basic_stmt.s8.asm leaves a gap before later code continues at 0x9700.
basic_data_cmd.s8.asm is placed at 0xC000 to reduce growth pressure near 0x9600.
